questions.prompt: Why do fewer campaigns usually beat many in 2026?

choices.text: Consolidation wins on three axes: (1) each ad set reaches the 50-conversions/week learning threshold instead of all fragmenting below it, (2) less audience overlap and auction self-competition, (3) clearer signal to read and easier hygiene. Minimum viable account for a DTC brand: one ASC prospecting + one retargeting + one brand/seasonal. Add structure only for genuine reasons (different margins, different geos, different objectives), not for reporting granularity — use breakdowns for that.

questions.explanation: Consolidation = learning threshold + less overlap + cleaner signal.

questions.prompt: Account has 28 active campaigns, most under $40/day. CPA broadly poor. Restructure?

choices.text: Consolidate aggressively. Most of those campaigns are below learning-phase volume (<50 conv/week each), so none has stable EAR predictions. Merge into one ASC prospecting at consolidated budget, one retargeting, one brand/seasonal — total 3-5 campaigns. Concentrating budget pushes each surviving campaign over the learning threshold. Expect short-term disruption as you cut/merge; medium-term CPA usually improves materially because the algorithm finally has signal it can act on.

questions.explanation: 28 campaigns below learning threshold = no signal. Consolidate to 3-5.
